title: Wholesome Turkish Yogurt Flatbread
description: A tangy, fluffy flatbread baked in a wood-fired oven, topped with fresh gem lettuce and a drizzle of yogurt sauce, perfect for a post-workout boost.
instructions: Mix all-purpose flour, active dry yeast, and salt in a bowl.
Add warm water and olive oil to the dry mix and stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 6 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise for 1 hour until doubled.
Punch down the dough, divide it into 4 equal portions, and roll each into a thin flat circle.
Heat a wood-fired pizza stone or cast-iron skillet to high temperature.
Place each flatbread on the hot surface and bake for 3-4 minutes per side until lightly golden.
Mix plain low fat yogurt, minced garlic, and lemon juice to create a creamy sauce.
Slide each flatbread onto a plate, top with fresh lettuce, drizzle yogurt sauce, and sprinkle sumac.
Serve immediately hot for a post-workout meal.
